Title: Kauniolide synthase is a P450 with unusual hydroxylation and cyclization-elimination activity

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of germacranolide to guaianolide branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step / branch-forming step downstream of costunolide
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of costunolide to kauniolide via unusual C3 hydroxylation (predominantly 3α-hydroxylation), followed by water elimination, cyclization, and regioselective deprotonation; also releases minor 3β-hydroxycostunolide side product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed enzyme toward guaianolides; key branch-forming/control step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Guaianolides have biological and pharmaceutical properties, including anticancer, anti-inflammatory, anti-mycobacterial, anti-parasitic, and antioxidant-response activating activities
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Ovary/flower tissues in feverfew inferred from pathway-gene expression and sesquiterpene lactone occurrence; heterologous accumulation in N. benthamiana leaves and yeast cultures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of the guaianolide kauniolide from costunolide and establishes the bridge from germacranolide to guaianolide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression-guided candidate selection; yeast microsome feeding assays with costunolide/parthenolide; LC-Orbitrap-FTMS; cysteine conjugation and co-injection with kauniolide-cysteine standard; feeding assay with chemically synthesized 3α-hydroxycostunolide; GC-MS; heterologous pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana and yeast; in silico docking/modeling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression in feverfew ovaries clusters with TpGAS, TpGAO, TpCOS and TpPTS during ovary development; validated by RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed heterologously in yeast microsomes and in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Yeast galactose-inducible promoter in pYEDP60/pESC system; Rubisco small subunit promoter (RBC) in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Microsome / endoplasmic reticulum-associated P450 (inferred from microsomal assays and P450 nature)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast galactose induction; transient agroinfiltration in N. benthamiana; in vitro feeding with costunolide, parthenolide, or 3α-hydroxycostunolide
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No specific growth or yield penalty reported; N. benthamiana mainly accumulated conjugated kauniolide-cysteine rather than free kauniolide
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enables metabolic engineering and heterologous production of guaianolide-type sesquiterpene lactones; useful for pathway reconstruction and bioengineering of valuable guaianolides such as artecanin
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned CYP71BZ6X. Does not accept parthenolide as substrate. 3β-hydroxycostunolide is not converted further by TpKLS, supporting stereospecific 3α-hydroxy intermediate route. Paper notes modelling-based mechanistic inference and absence of crystal structure
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 454 sequencing of trichome-enriched cDNA library; Illumina RNA-seq of six ovary developmental stages; dataset available from corresponding author on request
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tp8879 encodes kauniolide synthase (TpKLS), a CYP71 P450 from feverfew. 2. TpKLS converts costunolide directly into the guaianolide kauniolide, with minor formation of 3β-hydroxycostunolide. 3. TpKLS does not use parthenolide as substrate, establishing costunolide as the natural precursor. 4. Chemically synthesized 3α-hydroxycostunolide is converted to kauniolide by TpKLS, supporting it as the true intermediate. 5. TpKLS alone performs hydroxylation plus dehydration/cyclization-elimination chemistry, making it the first committed step toward guaianolides. 6. The pathway was reconstructed successfully in N. benthamiana and yeast.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Candidate cytochrome P450 (assigned CYP71DD5; kauniolide oxidase in Data availability, but activity not established here)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Putative downstream oxidation branch of sesquiterpene lactones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: A product was detected in yeast microsome assays, but could not be identified due to low abundance; catalytic role not established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate selection by co-expression and yeast microsome functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression in ovaries clusters with known sesquiterpene lactone pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Yeast expression vector pYEDP60 under galactose-inducible system
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast galactose induction; microsome feeding assays with costunolide or parthenolide
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Currently limited because enzymatic function remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned CYP71DD5. Although Data availability labels Tp8886 as kauniolide oxidase, the Results state the product could not be identified and no nutrient-metabolism causality was demonstrated
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 454 cDNA sequencing; Illumina RNA-seq of ovary development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tp8886 was selected as a co-expressed CYP71 candidate. 2. Yeast microsome assays produced a low-abundance product. 3. The product and enzyme function were not identified in this study, so its metabolic role remains unresolved.
